feed.title: Mozilla

entry.title: Performing a hard reload on a page (holding Shift while clicking the Refresh button) now also...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / New / Bug 418144 Performing a hard reload on a page (holding Shift while clicking the Refresh button) now also clears and updates its cached favicons.

entry.title: Firefox now gives Windows users the ability to change their default browser icon in Settings...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / New / Bug 2053546 Firefox now gives Windows users the ability to change their default browser icon in Settings (except for MSIX installs). Support for MSIX installs, macOS and Linux is coming soon.

entry.title: Announcing Rust 1.97.1
entry.content:
The Rust team has published a new point release of Rust, 1.97.1. Rust is a programming language that is empowering everyone to build reliable and efficient software. If you have a previous version of Rust installed via rustup, getting Rust 1.97.1 is as easy as: rustup update stable If you don't have it already, you can get rustup from the appropriate page on our website.
What's in 1.97.1 Rust 1.97.1 fixes a miscompilation in an LLVM optimization. We have backported both an LLVM fix and a disabl
entry.dc:creator: Planet Mozilla

entry.title: Firefox now supports the text-box-trim and text-box-edge CSS properties, and the associated...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2050141 Firefox now supports the text-box-trim and text-box-edge CSS properties, and the associated text-box shorthand. These properties allow you to control the leading space above and below text, enabling simpler and consistent vertical spacing and alignment.
entry.dc:creator: Nightly Release Notes

entry.title: Battery packs: Let's talk about crates, baby
entry.content: This blog post describes an idea I’ve been kicking around called battery packs. Battery packs are a curated set of crates arranged around a common theme. For example, there’s a CLI battery pack that has everything you need to build a great CLI, an opinionated pack for creating a backend web service, and one for embedded development (based on the Embedded Working Group’s Awesome Rust repository). We’ve also got some smaller ones, such as the error-handling battery pack that shows how to handle er
entry.dc:creator: Planet Mozilla

entry.title: Over The Edge 2.0: what independent researchers found about browser choice on Windows
entry.content: Two years ago Mozilla asked two leading experts on deceptive design, Dr. Harry Brignull and Cennydd Bowles, to look at how Microsoft was treating people who tried to use a different browser on Windows. Their report, Over The Edge, documented a pattern of design choices in Windows, Edge, and Bing that nudged, pressured, and at […] The post Over The Edge 2.0: what independent researchers found about browser choice on Windows appeared first on The Mozilla Blog .
entry.dc:creator: Mozilla Blog

entry.title: crates.io: development update
entry.content:
Another six months have passed since our last development update, and the crates.io team has been busy. Here's a summary of the most notable changes and improvements made to crates.io since then.
Source Code Viewer Crate pages now have a "Code" tab that lets you browse the contents of published crate versions directly on crates.io. This shows you the exact files that cargo downloads when you add a crate as a dependency, which might differ from the linked repository. This makes it much easier to
entry.dc:creator: Planet Mozilla

entry.title: how to change default browser on mac
entry.content: To find out how to change the default browser on a Mac , launch System Preferences, then go to Desktop & Dock (or General for older versions of macOS). Find the Default web browser section, select your desired browser from the list of options, like Safari, Google Chrome, Firefox, and Microsoft Edge, and the new default browser will open all future web links.
entry.dc:creator: Mozilla Connect

entry.title: Engineering Effectiveness Newsletter (Q2 2026 Edition)
entry.content:
Welcome to the Q2 edition of the Engineering Effectiveness Newsletter! The Engineering Effectiveness org makes it easy to develop, test and release Mozilla software at scale. See below for some highlights, then read on for more detailed info! Highlights
Improved mach startup overhead by 30-50%, as well as a 75% improvement for mach test on Windows and 10s faster configure for subsequent runs
Moved to weekly scheduled dot releases and faster rollouts, allowing us to deliver fixes and uplifts

entry.title: Added support for the No-Vary-Search HTTP response header for the HTTP disk cache. Servers can...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2038013 Added support for the No-Vary-Search HTTP response header for the HTTP disk cache. Servers can now declare which URL query parameters are irrelevant to the response, allowing Firefox to reuse cached resources across parameter variations and improving cache hit rates.
entry.dc:creator: Nightly Release Notes

entry.title: Mozilla Update Release - Inconvenient
entry.content: When in the middle of the day when I have 20 browser windows open and they all freeze so that Firefox can download a new release, that discombobulates the items I was working on. I should be notified and allowed to defer the update until I am ready to shut everything down. Please follow common change control rules. You want a reputation of being courteous. When you do not, it can affect profit and loss for day traders, and many other professions. Thank you for your attention and consideration.

entry.title: Netflix forces logout/crash when trying to play any video content
entry.content: Hello, I am experiencing an issue with Netflix on Zen Browser (Desktop). I can log in to my account without any issues and browse the catalog. However, the moment I click "Play" on any movie or TV show, an error occurs ("Si è verificato un problema" / An error occurred) and Zen immediately logs me out of my Netflix account. This seems to be related to DRM (Widevine) handshaking or Advanced Tracking Protection/Fingerprinting resistance blocking the video stream session tokens. OS: Windows 11

entry.title: Announcing Rust 1.96.1
entry.content:
The Rust team has published a new point release of Rust, 1.96.1. Rust is a programming language that is empowering everyone to build reliable and efficient software. If you have a previous version of Rust installed via rustup, getting Rust 1.96.1 is as easy as: rustup update stable If you don't have it already, you can get rustup from the appropriate page on our website.
What's in 1.96.1 Rust 1.96.1 fixes:
Missing retries / timeouts in Cargo's HTTP client Miscompilation in a MIR optimization
entry.dc:creator: Planet Mozilla

entry.title: May in Servo: user scripts, mp4 compat, blackboxing in DevTools, and more!
entry.content:
Servo 0.3.0 contains all of the changes we landed in May, which came out to 391 commits (March: 534). For security fixes, see § Security.
We’ve shipped several new web platform features:
‘font-kerning: none’ (@simonwuelker, #44634) ‘font-variant-east-asian’ (@simonwuelker, #44989) ‘font-variant-ligatures’ (@simonwuelker, #44903) ‘font-variant-numeric’ (@simonwuelker, #44950) ‘font-variant-position’ (@simonwuelker, #45142) mp4 files without fast start in (@calvaris, #45084) (@yezhizhen, #4502

entry.title: Will Mozilla stop using Mullvad for their VPN backend following the news that one of their founders is a major backer for a far right political activist group?

I recently read about the update where Mozilla VPN was integrated into Firefox and I want to be certain that none of my information is being sent to them. I also don't want to financially support Mullvad, even inadvertently, by supporting Mozilla if there is no plan put in place to end this partnership within a reasonable time frame.

entry.title: Starting with Firefox 154, Firefox Nightly now supports the text-box-trim and text-box-edge CSS...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2050138 Starting with Firefox 154, Firefox Nightly now supports the text-box-trim and text-box-edge CSS properties, and the associated text-box shorthand. These properties allow you to control the leading space above and below text, enabling simpler and consistent vertical spacing and alignment.
entry.dc:creator: Nightly Release Notes

entry.title: Starting with Firefox 154, Firefox Nightly now supports QUIC version 2 (RFC 9369) for HTTP/3...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / New / Bug 2040450 Starting with Firefox 154, Firefox Nightly now supports QUIC version 2 ( RFC 9369 ) for HTTP/3 connections. Firefox offers both QUIC v1 and v2 using compatible version negotiation ( RFC 9368 ). The server selects which version is used, so connections upgrade to QUIC v2 when the server chooses it and remain on QUIC v1 otherwise.
entry.dc:creator: Nightly Release Notes
